title: SEO Specialist — Website + Personal Brand (Full-Time, Long-Term)
description:
About the Role



I'm hiring a full-time SEO Specialist to run SEO for my business (AllWin Academy, a trading education platform) and my personal brand as the founder and published author of Currency of Mindset.



Two tracks:



1. Website SEO (65%) — On-page, off-page, and technical SEO for 
. Content briefs, meta, schema, backlinks, keyword tracking, Core Web Vitals, GSC monitoring.



2. Personal Brand SEO (35%) — Rank my name on Google. Build controlled SERP real estate (author bio pages, schema, entity building, Google Knowledge Panel work, book SEO across Amazon, Goodreads, and Google).



This is NOT a content writing role, social media role, or outreach role. Pure SEO execution and strategy.





What You'll Own



- Full SEO audit and 90-day roadmap in Month 1

- Monthly keyword ranking reports with before/after screenshots

- Backlink building through legitimate outreach (no PBNs, no spam, no link farms)

- WordPress edits (Editor role first 30 days, Admin access after trust is built)

- Technical SEO — site speed, crawl errors, schema markup, mobile usability, Core Web Vitals

- Weekly Friday audit + monthly strategy report





Requirements to Apply



- 2+ years of real SEO experience — not just a course or certificate

- Portfolio of at least 2 sites you've personally ranked, with screenshots from Ahrefs, SEMrush, or Google Search Console showing ranking movement you drove

- Solid written English — you'll be writing meta descriptions, content briefs, and outreach
s

- Proficient with Ahrefs or SEMrush, Google Search Console, Google Analytics, Screaming Frog, and WordPress

- Working knowledge of technical SEO, schema markup (JSON-LD), and Core Web Vitals

- Able to work independently without hand-holding

- Reliable internet, quiet work environment, available on WhatsApp during work hours





Bonus Points



- Experience with personal brand SEO or reputation management

- Experience ranking content in finance, trading, or education niches

- Familiarity with WordPress theme structure (no coding required, just awareness)





What I'm Looking For



- Someone who thinks like an operator, not a task-rabbit

- Honest communication — if something's not working, say it early

- Ownership over outcomes, not just hours logged

- Zero tolerance for black-hat tactics, fake backlinks, or inflated reports





Compensation



- $650/mo flat rate during the 30-day paid trial

- Compensation review after Month 1 based on performance

- Performance-based bonus structure discussed after trial period

- Paid monthly on the 1st, on time, every month

- Quarterly reviews with raise eligibility





Schedule



- Full-time, 40 hrs/week

- Full KPIs from day one — no ramp-up

- Work whatever hours fit your life — only outcomes and KPIs matter

- Saturdays and Sundays off





How to Apply



Send a reply with all five of the following. Applications missing any of these will be ignored.



1. A 3-sentence intro — who you are and what you've done

2. Links or screenshots of 2 sites you've ranked, with before/after data

3. Your answer to this prompt: "I run a WordPress site with 40 pages. Organic traffic has been flat for 6 months, Domain Rating is 12. Walk me through what you'd check in the first 48 hours."

4. Confirmation you accept the posted rate of $650/mo for the 30-day trial period

5. Earliest start date



Note: I read every application personally. If your reply looks copy-pasted or skips the 48-hour question, it goes straight to the trash. If you're the right fit, you'll hear back within 5 days.

title: Google Ads Pmax Expert
description:
we have a direct-to-consumer solid-wood furniture brand operating across the UK and US. We make heirloom pieces — the kind customers keep for decades — and we sell them online at a scale most luxury furniture brands can't match.We're looking for a Performance Max specialist who lives and breathes Google's ad stack and knows how to make PMax actually perform on a large, high-AOV catalogue. This isn't a generalist paid media role. We want someone who has wrestled PMax into submission for a furniture, home, or considered-purchase brand and has the account screenshots to prove it.What you'll ownYou'll take full ownership of our Google Ads account — PMax first, but also Shopping, Search, and YouTube where they support the machine. The catalogue is large and constantly evolving, which means feed quality, asset group structure, and signal strategy are where the work actually gets done.Specifically:– Architecting PMax campaigns the right way for a luxury furniture catalogue — asset group segmentation by category, margin, and AOV band, not lazy "one campaign for everything"

– Merchant Center and feed optimisation: titles, attributes, custom labels, supplemental feeds, GTIN hygiene

– Audience signal strategy: customer lists, first-party data, in-market, custom segments — feeding the algorithm the right priors

– Creative asset production workflow: briefing, testing, and rotating image/video assets at the pace PMax demands

– Bidding and budget strategy across tROAS targets, new customer acquisition goals, and seasonal peaks

– Search term insights, brand vs. non-brand separation, and negative strategy to stop PMax cannibalising organic and brand traffic

– Reporting that actually tells us something — asset-level performance, category-level ROAS, incrementality where possibleWhat we're looking for– 3+ years running Google Ads at scale, with at least 18 months deep in Performance Max

– Direct experience with large product catalogues (500+ SKUs) in furniture, home, luxury, or considered-purchase verticals

– High-AOV context matters — you understand why a £2,000 dining table behaves nothing like a £40 t-shirt in PMax

– Fluent with Google Merchant Center, feed management tools (GoDataFeed, Feedonomics, DataFeedWatch, or similar), and GA4

– Comfortable reading the signals PMax doesn't show you and building your own reporting layer

– Strong opinions on PMax vs. Standard Shopping, when to break PMax apart, and how to stop Google spending your budget badly

title: SEO Manager
description:
SEO Manager — Premium Baby Brands (Formuland + )

Remote • Full-time contractor • 40,000 –60,000 /mo php • Timezone: Pacific (LA)

About us

Inc. operates two premium baby brands:, a US retailer of European organic baby formula (HiPP, Holle, Kendamil, Aptamil) serving 50,000+ parents and generating ~100K monthly organic traffic;

The role

We're hiring an SEO Manager to own organic growth across both brands. You'll report directly to the founder and lead a small SEO team (two content writers, one developer). Your job is to turn our existing strategy into measurable monthly traffic and revenue growth through technical SEO, content direction, and relentless measurement.

This is not a writing role. You'll be writing technical tickets for developers, content briefs for writers, and monthly reports for leadership — but you won't be writing blog posts yourself. If you want a writing job, this isn't it.

What you'll own

Monthly (not quarterly) SEO performance reporting across both brands — traffic, rankings, CTR, indexation, Core Web Vitals, SERP feature presence

Competitor monitoring — continuous, not periodic. You'll know within a week when a competitor moves, not a quarter later

Technical SEO audits — monthly Screaming Frog crawls, Lighthouse audits on top 20 pages, GSC enhancement report reviews

Content strategy — you d
 what we write, when we refresh, and how posts cluster. You write the briefs; our writers execute

Developer handoffs — you write technical tickets (schema, Liquid templates, performance) for our Shopify developer Ayush and QA every deployment

Internal linking & topic cluster architecture across both domains

AI Overview optimization — content restructuring for extraction, FAQPage schema rollout, source citation strategy

KPI dashboard — a single source of truth you update weekly that the founder reviews every Monday

Must-have qualifications

3+ years managing SEO for an e-commerce site (Shopify strongly preferred)

Hands-on proficiency with Screaming Frog, Google Search Console, Semrush (or Ahrefs), PageSpeed Insights, and Lighthouse

Proven ability to read and write JSON-LD schema markup (Product, FAQPage, Article, BreadcrumbList) — you can identify a broken schema from the source code

Experience with Shopify Liquid — enough to write specs for a developer, not necessarily to code production-ready templates yourself

Track record of recovering traffic from a Google algorithm update, AI Overview rollout, or similar platform shift (you'll be asked about this specifically)

Strong written English — you'll be writing briefs and reports daily

Comfortable working autonomously with weekly check-ins — we don't micromanage, but we expect ownership

Nice-to-have

Experience in YMYL niches (health, baby, medical, finance)

Familiarity with AI Overview / Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) strategy

Background in premium DTC or US e-commerce

Experience managing remote content writers

Working knowledge of GA4, Looker Studio, or similar reporting tools

What we offer

$1,200–$1,500 USD/month (probation $1,000–$1,200, post-probation full rate) — paid via Wise

Full-time, long-term contractor position (this is not a project gig)

Flexible hours with at least 3 hours overlap with Pacific time for weekly Monday reviews

Paid time off — 1 week vacation (your choice) + 3 sick days annually + Formuland-observed holidays

Direct access to the founder — no middle management between you and decision-making

A documented strategy (not a blank slate) — we've already done the diagnosis and built a 28-task playbook

How to apply

Send a short
 answering these three questions (do NOT send a generic resume):

Link to one e-commerce SEO project where you made a measurable traffic impact. Include the starting baseline, the specific changes you made, and the result.

Describe a specific technical SEO problem you diagnosed and fixed. We want detail — tools used, what you found, what you changed, how you validated the fix.

What's your take on AI Overviews — threat, opportunity, both? How would you approach it for a site losing CTR to them?

Applications without answers to all three questions will not be reviewed. We're hiring for judgment, not credentials.

title: Senior AI Systems Architect
description:
Senior AI Systems Architect (Contract-Based)



Build a Private AI Brain for a Knowledge-Driven Organization



We are not looking for a prompt engineer.

We are not looking for someone who “uses ChatGPT well.”



We are looking for a senior AI systems architect who can design and implement a structured, private AI assistant trained strictly on our internal knowledge base.



If you have not built real AI systems before, this role is not for you.



THE MISSION



We are a knowledge-intensive organization with extensive proprietary educational, analytical, and operational materials.



We want to build a private AI assistant that:



-Understands our internal frameworks and methodologies

-Synthesizes information across multiple documents

-Assists in research and structured analysis

-Drafts outputs aligned with our internal logic

-Operates within strict guardrails

-Uses only our curated materials unless explicitly instructed



This is not basic document search.

This is building a controlled AI research and analysis system.



WHAT YOU WILL BUILD



-A structured AI knowledge architecture

-A private internal AI research assistant

-A system capable of synthesis, analysis, and structured output

-Proper retrieval and reasoning workflows

-Guardrails to minimize hallucination and knowledge drift

-Clear documentation so our team can use and maintain the system



If your solution is “just upload everything into ChatGPT,” this role is not for you.



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE



You must have real experience with:



-OpenAI API

-Building AI systems for real clients or companies

-Retrieval-based systems (RAG or similar frameworks)

-Working with large structured and unstructured data

-Designing systems, not just writing prompts



Bonus if familiar with:



-Gemini

-NotebookLM

-AI workflow automation

-Knowledge base architecture



We are looking for builders and system thinkers.



WHO SHOULD NOT APPLY



-Prompt engineers without backend experience

-VAs who simply use AI tools

-Applicants without proof of real-world AI systems built



ENGAGEMENT STRUCTURE



-Contract / project-based

-Milestone-driven

-Potential ongoing retainer relationship based on performance



HOW TO APPLY



To be considered, you must complete the application form below:

title: Project Manager
description:
We are seeking a Project Manager with at least 2 years of project management experience. The ideal candidate will have a background in Agile and Sprint methodologies. While experience in senior living is a plus, it is not a requirement.



Key Responsibilities:

• Lead and manage project deliverables to deliver project on time and within scope.

• Develop and maintain project plans, and schedule.

• Facilitate Agile/Sprint planning sessions and ensure adherence to Agile principles.

• Collaborate with stakeholders to define project requirements and deliverables.

• Monitor project progress and make adjustments as necessary to ensure successful project completion.

• Communicate project status, risks, and issues to stakeholders in a timely manner.

Qualifications:

• Minimum of 2 years of project management experience.

• Experience with Agile and Sprint methodologies.

• Strong organizational and leadership skills.

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

• Experience with Azure DevOps is helpful but not required.

Preferred Qualifications:

• Experience in the senior living industry.

title: Remote Construction Takeoff Technician / Quantity Surveyor Assistant
description:
We are a U.S.-based construction company looking for a remote construction takeoff assistant to perform quantity takeoffs using the PlanSwift online estimating platform.



Responsibilities include measuring quantities from construction drawings, marking up plans, preparing Excel/Google Sheet summaries, listing drawings reviewed, and identifying unclear scope items or questions.



Typical scopes may include painting, drywall, framing, flooring, ceiling tile/grid, selective demolition, concrete, doors, openings, and basic item counts.



Applicant must have experience reading construction plans and performing quantity takeoffs. PlanSwift experience is strongly preferred. Experience with U.S. commercial construction drawings is a plus.



Each assignment must include marked-up plans, quantity summaries, drawing references, and open questions. Accuracy and organization are more important than speed.



Applicants must provide sample takeoff work, screenshots, or marked-up plans.



Please include your PlanSwift experience, construction takeoff background, availability, desired rate, and sample work.

title: Ad Creative Strategist / Copywriter (E-commerce Ads) – Remote
description:
Ad Creative Strategist / Copywriter (E-commerce Ads) – Remote (India / Philippines)



Role Overview



We are looking for an Ad Creative Strategist / Copywriter who can lead the direction of our ad creatives (image + video).



This is not a design role.

You will define:



what ads to create

what message to communicate

how the visuals should look

You will work with our designer and PPC team to create ads that drive CTR, conversions, and revenue.



What You’ll Do



Develop ad angles and hooks based on customer psychology

Write high-converting ad copy (Google Ads, Meta Ads)

Create clear creative briefs for designers and video editors

Define visual direction (scenes, layout, text overlay, tone)

Structure video ads (Hook ? Problem ? Solution ? Proof ? CTA)

Plan and run creative A/B testing

Analyze performance (CTR, CVR, ROAS) and iterate

Collaborate with internal team (design, PPC)

What We’re Looking ForMust Have



Experience in direct response ads (Google Ads or Meta Ads)

Strong copywriting skills (short-form, conversion-focused)

Ability to create ad concepts, not just write text

Understanding of PPC metrics (CTR, conversion rate, ROAS)

Experience giving clear direction to designers or editors

Nice to Have



E-commerce / DTC experience

Baby, health, or premium product experience

Experience with video ad scripting (short-form)

What This Role Is NOT



Not a graphic designer

Not a video editor

Not a general marketing role

This is a performance-driven creative role focused on ads.



Example of Expected Work



You should be able to take:



“Premium baby diaper made for sensitive skin”



And turn it into:



Hook

Headline

Ad copy

Visual direction

Video concept

Location & Work Setup



Remote (India or Philippines preferred)

Must have overlap with US Pacific Time (Los Angeles)

Based on experience

Please include your expected monthly salary (USD)

what college major you have?

Bachelor's (Preferred)

title: Recruiting & Onboarding Assistant (Support to Recruiting Manager)
description:
Recruiting & Onboarding Assistant

Full-Time | Remote | TimeProof Required | GoHighLevel + ATS Experience Required



We are a U.S.-based commercial services company building a structured, accountable internal hiring and onboarding operation. We are hiring a strong Recruiting & Onboarding Assistant to support our Recruiting Manager and help keep recruiting workflows, candidate communication, onboarding steps, and documentation fully organized, accurate, and moving forward.



This is not a basic assistant role.

This is not a passive support position.



We need someone who is highly organized, process-driven, detail-focused, and capable of taking ownership of recruiting coordination, onboarding follow-up, system updates, and documentation control. The right person must be able to keep things on track, maintain clean records, and step in with confidence when structure is needed.



This role is execution-focused and requires strong follow-through, responsiveness, and the ability to help take pressure off leadership by managing the details correctly.



Core Responsibilities



1) Recruiting Support

	•	Post and maintain job ads across platforms

	•	Source applicants through job boards, databases, and social channels

	•	Review resumes and complete initial screening steps

	•	Schedule interviews and maintain clear candidate notes

	•	Manage candidate follow-ups by SMS,
, and phone inside GoHighLevel

	•	Keep candidates moving through the pipeline with accurate statuses and timely follow-up

	•	Help ensure no qualified candidate is left sitting without action



2) Onboarding Support

	•	Coordinate onboarding steps from start to finish

	•	Send required forms, links, and instructions and confirm completion

	•	Communicate expectations, next steps, and start details clearly

	•	Track onboarding and training progress in GoHighLevel and the ATS

	•	Follow up on missing items until everything is complete

	•	Confirm readiness before candidates are activated, scheduled, or cleared forward



3) Compliance and Documentation

	•	Collect and organize W-9/W-4 forms, IDs, licenses, certifications, and required onboarding documents

	•	Store files correctly and maintain complete digital records

	•	Keep tags, tasks, notes, and checklists accurate and up to date

	•	Help ensure all files are complete, organized, and audit-ready

	•	Identify missing documentation quickly and follow through until resolved



4) Client and Candidate Communication

	•	Make calls as needed to confirm interview availability, onboarding status, scheduling, start requirements, and hiring needs

	•	Communicate professionally with both candidates and internal stakeholders

	•	Document outcomes, next steps, and follow-up actions inside the system

	•	Help keep communication clear, timely, and organized across the process



5) Process Support and SOP Execution

	•	Maintain and update SOPs, checklists, and recruiting/onboarding workflows

	•	Help improve workflow structure by documenting what is working and what is being missed

	•	Create and maintain templates for repeatable steps such as follow-ups, checklists, and candidate messaging

	•	Support the Recruiting Manager by helping bring consistency and order to day-to-day execution



6) Systems Coordination and Reporting

	•	Keep pipelines, tasks, follow-ups, notes, and statuses current in GoHighLevel

	•	Maintain ATS accuracy and ensure candidate records are up to date

	•	Provide weekly updates and status reporting to the Recruiting Manager

	•	Help ensure nothing is missed, delayed, or left incomplete

	•	Be capable of stepping in and keeping operations moving when the manager is unavailable or overloaded



What We Need in This Role



We are looking for someone who can do more than just assist. This person needs to be strong enough to help hold the process together, keep workflows clean, and take over key coordination tasks without constant hand-holding.



The right candidate must be:

	•	Highly organized

	•	Strong in follow-up

	•	Detail-focused

	•	Comfortable with structure and process

	•	Confident handling communication by phone,
, and text

	•	Able to keep multiple moving parts organized at once

	•	Reliable, responsive, and consistent

	•	Capable of taking initiative when something is missing or off track



Required Experience



You must already have hands-on experience with:

	•	GoHighLevel, including pipelines, tags, tasks, notes, forms, and workflows

	•	An ATS or recruiting CRM such as Zoho Recruit, CEIPAL, Bullhorn, Tracker, JobAdder, or similar

	•	Google Workspace, including Docs and Sheets

	•	Recruiting support, onboarding coordination, or hiring operations

	•	Documentation tracking and file organization

	•	Candidate communication and scheduling



Non-Negotiables

	•	Strong organization and follow-through

	•	Clear spoken and written English

	•	Comfortable with phone communication

	•	Able to follow defined processes and maintain clean records

	•	Reliable, responsive, and consistent during EST work hours

	•	TimeProof tracking required

	•	Must already understand how to work inside systems without creating confusion or missed steps



Schedule and Tracking

	•	Full-time, 40 hours per week

	•	Weekly meeting plus quick check-ins as needed

	•	TimeProof required, including screenshots and activity logs



To Apply



All 3 items below are required. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



1) Video Introduction (2 to 4 minutes)



Please cover:

	•	Your recruiting or onboarding background

	•	Your experience with GoHighLevel

	•	Your ATS experience

	•	Your experience with candidate communication and phone follow-up

	•	Your documentation and onboarding process experience

	•	An example of a checklist, SOP, or workflow you have supported or maintained



2) Resume



3) Systems List



Please list the tools and systems you have personally used.

title: AI Ads & Content Creator — Full Stack Operator
description:
Looking for someone to own ads and organic content end-to-end. You'll run the whole creative operation — copy, video, publishing, tracking — using AI tools I've already set up.

This is not a traditional editor or content role. I've built an AI creative system — curated prompts for copy, AI video tools for production. I need a sharp operator who can execute that system at volume, catch AI failure modes before they ship, and keep the output pipeline moving.

What you'll actually do:



Generate ad and content copy using our internal AI prompts (you run it, I review)

Produce video using Higgsfield, Kling, Runway, Veo, etc. — whatever fits the brief

Edit, cut, caption, and pace videos for Meta and organic social

Kill bad AI outputs — weird hands, uncanny faces, audio drift, off-brand feel

Publish across Meta Ads Manager and organic channels

Own naming conventions, asset organization, and content calendar

Track performance in a shared sheet so we know what's working

Iterate fast — I care about volume of tested ideas, not polish



What you need:



Solid video editing, 2+ years (CapCut or Premiere)

Real hands-on experience with AI video tools — Higgsfield, Kling, Runway, Pika, Veo. If you've never touched these, this role isn't for you

Sharp eye for what feels native in feed vs. what feels like generic AI slop

Strong written English — enough to catch copywriting nuance

Comfortable publishing and managing Meta Ads Manager

Organized, self-directed, takes feedback without getting precious about it



Nice to have:



Direct-response ad experience (not brand/awareness)

Basic motion graphics or sound design

Experience running content calendars across multiple channels



Setup:



Full-time, long-term

Flexible hours with some US Eastern overlap for reviews

13th month, paid time off



To apply:



Start your message with the word kanban so I know you read this

2–3 work samples, ideally with AI-generated elements

Tell me which AI tools you actually use and your honest take on each

Skip the template cover letter — just be real

Bonus: include a favorite ad or piece of content (yours or anyone's) and why it worked



Wage/Salary: $700–$800/month

Desired Hours per Week: 40

Business or Contact Name: Rob

title: Digital Marketer
description:
Atlas Medical is seeking a proactive Digital Marketer to join our team and drive inbound lead generation in the healthcare B2B sector. This remote role focuses on executing practical online marketing activities 
 campaigns, LinkedIn content, SEO, blog writing, backlinks, etc.) to keep our sales funnel full. 



Our digital marketer will need to  bring specialized marketing expertise. You must be adept in areas like LinkedIn management, content marketing, SEO,
 campaigns, and analytics to expand Atlas’s visibility and capture qualified healthcare buyer inquiries. 



The ideal candidate will be detail-oriented, highly organized, and able to work independently during Eastern Time business hours with live responsiveness. Availability and fast response times are critical.



Role Summary

The Digital Marketer will work closely with Atlas Medical’s owner to increase qualified inbound opportunities. Core duties include: planning and sending
 blasts; creating and scheduling LinkedIn posts; managing SEO execution (keyword research, on-page updates); writing and publishing blogs; building and tracking backlinks; and optimizing website conversion performance. In essence, this person will handle hands-on marketing tasks that grow Atlas’s pipeline – for example, writing and post blogs (via our Duda platform), coordinating
 campaigns (using
Octopus), and following up on digital leads.



Key Responsibilities: 

- Plan, draft, and send professional
 campaigns (design, copy, list segmentation) using
Octopus

- Create, post, and engage on LinkedIn to boost brand awareness and find leads

- Perform SEO tasks: keyword research (Ubersuggest), optimize website pages (titles, meta, content), and improve organic rankings.

- Write and publish B2B content (blog posts, articles, case studies) to showcase Atlas’s expertise

- Develop backlink outreach plans (e.g. guest posts or PR) to strengthen domain authority.

- Monitor and report on digital marketing performance (inbound leads, MQLs, website metrics) and continuously refine tactics.



Required Skills & Qualifications

- B2B Marketing Expertise: Proven experience in digital marketing activities (social media, content writing,
 campaigns, SEO) – ideally in a B2B or technical field. Strong writing and editing skills are essential to craft clear, professional copy.



- Technical Proficiency: Comfortable using marketing software tools. We use the Duda website platform

 for site updates,
Octopus for
 marketing, Ubersuggest for SEO keyword research, plus LinkedIn for publishing content. You should be able to learn and navigate these tools (experience with them is a plus).



-Data & Analytics Mindset: Ability to track and interpret performance metrics (e.g. Google Analytics,
 open rates, keyword rankings). You’ll regularly report on key results (inbound inquiries, conversion rates, SEO rank improvements).



- Organization & Independence: Highly organized with strong attention to detail. We need someone who can take ownership of recurring tasks (weekly
, monthly blog, etc.) and follow through without constant supervision. Reliability and consistency are musts.



- Communication: Excellent written and verbal English skills. You must be responsive during work hours (fast replies to messages, timely updates on tasks). You must bereliable, consistent, and responsive during Eastern Standard Time workday

.

Preferred Experience

- Healthcare or Medical Devices: Background in healthcare, hospitals, or medical device marketing is a plus, given our focus on mobile imaging and cath lab rentals. 

- B2B Lead Gen: Experience generating qualified leads for B2B services or equipment.

- SEO & Content Marketing: Proven track record of improving site search rankings and engagement through content strategies.

-
 & CRM Tools: Prior experience with
 marketing platforms or CRMs.

- Graphic or Web Skills: Basic skills in design (Canva) and comfortable editing website content.



Tools & Platforms

The Digital Marketer will use Atlas’s existing marketing toolset. Familiarity with these or similar platforms is expected:



Duda – Our website builder

You’ll update blog pages and ensure blog updates go smoothly.



Octopus – Our
 marketing service

You’ll create and send newsletters, set up campaigns, and monitor
 analytics.



Ubersuggest – SEO/keyword research tool

Use it to find target keywords (e.g. “modular cath lab rentals”) and track rankings.



LinkedIn – Primary social channel for B2B content. We need to post regularly to engage healthcare executives. You should know how to publish posts/articles and manage a company page.



You may also use standard office/cloud tools (Google Sheets/Docs, Excel). Being tech-savvy and able to quickly learn new tools is important.



Hours & Availability

Eastern Time Work Hours: This is a remote role requiring live overlap with US Eastern Time business hours. The VA must work Weekday hours (Monday–Friday, ~9am–5pm ET) and be actively online for real-time communication. Candiates must be reachable on EST during the workweek (teams coordinate via
, whatsapp, and zoom).

Responsiveness: Rapid response to communications is critical.

The Digital Marketer should check in regularly, update on task status, and reply to queries promptly.

Workload: TBD. Will start as part time and ramp up as needed based on results.



Compensation

Format: We offer a competitive hourly rate based on experience.

Payment: Paid weekly.



How to Apply

Please provide the folloiwng:

Introduction: A brief cover letter explaining why you’re a strong fit for this role (mention relevant experience with
, SEO, content, etc.).



Resume: Highlighting your digital marketing and virtual assistant experience.



Portfolio/Work Samples: Links or attachments of relevant work (e.g. a LinkedIn post you wrote, an
 newsletter you designed, a blog article or SEO report).



Tools Experience: List the platforms you’ve used (especially Duda or other website builders,
Octopus or other
 tools, Ubersuggest/SEO tools).



Availability: Confirm your availability for Eastern Time hours (weekday)



Rate: Specify your hourly rate or monthly fee expectation.



Applications without clear confirmation of EST availability and response capabilities will not be considered. We are looking for someone dependable and long-term who can execute tasks autonomously while staying communicative.

title: Senior Performance Marketing Manager - Google Ads Focus
description:
TO APPLY: MUST INCLUDE LINKEDIN PROFILE AND RESUME



Company: Promofect



We’re a NY/NJ-based custom apparel and promotional products company looking for a senior performance marketer to take full ownership of our paid acquisition efforts.



Google Ads is our core channel, but this role goes far beyond campaign management. We need someone who can improve lead quality, reduce wasted spend, and drive consistent, scalable growth across the entire acquisition funnel.



We are looking for a full-time tea
mber who can manage the Google Ads process end-to-end, including video creation and optimization for ads.



What You’ll Do

- Own and optimize Google Ads performance end-to-end

- Identify underperforming areas quickly and implement fixes

- Build and refine keyword strategies based on intent, margins, and profitability

- Improve ad copy, creative, and offer positioning

- Align ads with landing pages to increase conversion rates

- Ensure accurate conversion tracking, attribution, and data integrity

- Optimize beyond campaigns, including funnel performance and lead quality

- Explore and scale YouTube and other paid channels

- Collaborate with SEO and marketing teams for full funnel growth

- Report performance with clear insights, not just metrics



What We’re Looking For

- 5 to 10 plus years of experience in Google Ads or performance marketing

- Proven track record of improving lead quality and lowering cost per lead

- Strong understanding of search intent, funnel strategy, and conversion optimization

- Experience with landing pages and CRO

- Ability to think beyond ads and into full acquisition strategy

- Strong analytical and decision making skills

- Direct communicator who can explain what’s working, what’s not, and why

- Experience with video editing for Google Ads, including YouTube and video creatives, is required



DO NOT APPLY IF

- You focus only on campaign setup and not ongoing performance

- You rely heavily on automation without clear strategy

- You cannot clearly explain why campaigns succeed or fail

- You are managing too many accounts to be fully engaged

- You do not have experience with video editing for Google Ads campaigns

- You previously worked with Promofect and did not meet performance expectations

title: Mortgage Broker Assistant (Mortgage Admin) – Part-Time (20 hrs/week, EST)
description:
Long-Term | Client-Facing | Growth Opportunity



We’re hiring a highly organized, detail-driven Mortgage Broker Assistant to support a successful Mortgage Broker in Ontario, Canada.



This role is for someone who thrives on structure, takes ownership, and genuinely cares about client experience. Your mission is simple: buy back the broker’s time by keeping files organized, ensuring documents are correct, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks — so the broker can focus on closing deals and growing the business.



What You’ll Own



File & Document Management

• Collect, organize, rename, and store client documents properly

• Review documents at a high level and flag missing/incorrect/outdated items

• Request corrections from clients clearly and professionally

• Keep every file complete and up to date (clean folders + consistent naming)



Mortgage File Support

• Prepare short deal summary notes for the broker (clear, concise, accurate)

• Prepare signing packages once approved

• Schedule signing review meetings with clients

• Track deadlines and follow-ups so files keep moving



Client Communication & Follow-Up

• Communicate with clients via
 and scheduling tools

• Follow up politely but firmly when documents or actions are outstanding

• Maintain a calm, professional client experience (even under time pressure)



Admin, Scheduling & Compliance Support

• Coordinate calendar scheduling during EST hours

• Support ID verification and basic document/compliance checks

• Maintain clean systems and adopt new tools quickly



Must-Haves

• Expert English communication (written + spoken)

• Extremely organized and detail-oriented

• Available for 20 hours/week in EST

• Strong ownership: you proactively track, follow up, and close loops

• Tech-comfortable: cloud storage, file systems, basic CRM/task tracking, e-sign tools, scheduling

• Professional, client-first mindset

• Reliable internet + quiet workspace suitable for client-facing work



Strong Preference (High Priority)

• Experience supporting a Canadian mortgage broker 

• Familiarity with Canadian mortgage documentation and broker workflows

(If you have this, please highlight it clearly in your application.)



Nice to Have

• Mortgage / real estate / banking / finance experience

• Experience reviewing documents or working in a compliance-driven environment

• EA / Ops / Admin support experience

• Familiarity with Google Workspace and e-signature platforms



Hours & Growth

• 20 hours/week (EST)

• Long-term role with potential to move full-time for the right candidate

• Opportunity to grow into a senior operations / file management role over time



HOW TO APPLY

• Send your resume

• Mention in 1–2 lines if you’ve supported a Canadian mortgage broker

• Confirm you can work EST

• IMPORTANT: Use this subject line: Mortgage EA – Canada – [Your Name]

title: Human Resources Manager
description:
We are a fast-growing Canadian security and operations company looking for a **hands-on, experienced HR Manager** who can step in immediately and take ownership of our HR function.



This is not a “build from scratch slowly” role — we need someone who **already understands HR in a Canadian context** and can confidently manage payroll, compliance, employee relations, and HR operations with minimal guidance.



You will work directly with leadership and play a key role in stabilizing and improving HR across the organization.



Key Responsibilities



1. HR Operations & Administration



* Take full ownership of day-to-day HR functions

* Maintain employee records and HR systems

* Manage onboarding and offboarding processes

* Ensure HR policies are followed and properly documented



**2. Payroll & Compliance**



* Oversee payroll processing (bi-weekly)

* Ensure compliance with **Canadian employment standards (Ontario preferred)**

* Handle statutory requirements (vacation pay, stat pay, ROEs, etc.)

* Coordinate with accounting and external providers as needed



**3. Employee Relations**



* Act as the primary HR point of contact for staff

* Handle employee issues, complaints, and disciplinary processes

* Support terminations, performance management, and documentation



**4. Recruitment & Staffing**



* Manage hiring processes (job postings, screening, coordination)

* Work with leadership to identify staffing needs

* Ensure efficient and high-quality hiring execution



**5. HR Systems & Process Improvement**



* Identify gaps and inefficiencies in current HR processes

* Implement structure, documentation, and best practices

* Help bring discipline and clarity to HR operations



Requirements (Non-Negotiable)**



* Strong understanding of **Canadian HR practices and employment laws**

* Proven experience managing HR independently (not just support roles)

* Experience with **payroll processing and compliance**

* Ability to **step in with minimal training and take ownership immediately**

* Strong communication skills (clear, direct, professional)

* Highly organized and detail-oriented

* Comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment



Nice to Have**



* Experience supporting Canadian companies remotely

* Background in security, staffing, or operations-heavy industries

* Experience cleaning up or rebuilding HR processes in messy environments



---



### **What We’re Looking For**



We are not looking for someone junior or theoretical.



We want someone who:



* Sees what’s broken and fixes it

* Doesn’t wait for instructions

* Brings structure, accountability, and clarity

* Can operate like a true **HR owner**, not just an administrator



---



### **Compensation**



* Competitive (based on experience)

* Long-term opportunity with growth potential



---



### **How to Apply**



Please include:



1. A brief summary of your experience with Canadian HR

2. Specific examples of payroll/compliance work you’ve handled

3. A short note on how quickly you can take over an HR function with minimal guidance



---



If you’re someone who can come in, take control, and make an immediate impact — we want to hear from you.

title: CapCut Video Editor
description:
Role Overview



We are looking for a detail-oriented and instruction-driven Video Editor to join our team at Exchange Life, a US-based fast growing travel jewelry brand, selling flag charms (Our website: 
). Your mission will be to create short, clean, high-performing organic videos for Instagram and TikTok, simple, native-looking content that blends into the feed and drives results.



These are fast edits: 7–15 second videos, simple text on screen, no effects or complex transitions. Each video takes around 15 minutes to make, while the focus needs to be on picking the best clips available from our clip library, which requires a good eye for aesthetics and visual quality.



If you have the eye for good aesthetics and have a knack for creating content that stops the scroll and engages audiences, we want to talk to you. This is a long-term, fully remote position for an editor who is passionate about the intersection of creativity and data, and is excited by the opportunity to have their work directly impact business results.



What You'll Do:



- Edit short videos (7–15 seconds) for organic TikTok and Instagram Reels using footage from our clip library.

- Select the strongest clips: the right 
nts, the right angles, the right feel.

- Keep edits clean: simple on-screen text, no heavy effects, natural pacing.

- Follow creative briefs closely and deliver consistently.

- Turn edits around quickly once an assignment comes in.



Who We're Looking For:



- A Strong Eye for Aesthetics: You can look at raw footage and immediately know what's aesthetic and what's not. Clip selection is the job, and you take it seriously.

- Precise and Instruction-Driven: You follow briefs closely, ask questions when something is unclear, and don't improvise when the direction is specific.

- Short-Form Native: Your recent work is TikTok and Reels, not YouTube, not long-form, not cinematic edits.

- Fast and Efficient: You move through this simple editing style quickly.

- CapCut or Premiere Pro Proficient: Comfortable in a fast-paced editing workflow.

- Reliable: Strong English, detail-oriented, and able to work independently with minimal back-and-forth.

- Available: Reliable internet and hardware to edit.



Compensation & Growth



We believe in rewarding top talent for outstanding results. This role offers a unique compensation structure designed to give you significant earning potential.



- Base Rate: You will start at 180 PHP per hour ($3/hour), averaging 6 hours of work a day, 6 days a week. Fully flexible, you manage your own time.

- Performance Bonuses: On top of your hourly rate, you will earn substantial monthly bonuses for creating viral videos and winning ads that meet or exceed performance targets. Our bonus structure is uncapped.

- Earning Potential: Our top-performing editors consistently earn $1,000-$1,500+ per month by combining base hours with performance bonuses.



This is a long-term position, not a gig. Most of our editors have been with us for over a year.



Why Join Our Team?



- Fully Remote: Work from anywhere in the world.

- No Fixed Schedule: You manage your own time. Deliver fast, and own your output.

- Direct Impact: See your creative work translate directly into business success and your own earnings.

- Growth & Stability: Join a team that values long-term relationships and provides ongoing training and opportunities.



Ready to Apply?



Complete our application form here: 



The form includes everything. Do not
 or message us directly. All applications must go through the form.

title: Medical Intake Coordinator
description:
We are seeking a detail-oriented and compassionate Medical Intake Coordinator to manage patient intake processes for our Hospice and Home Health services in California. This role serves as the primary point of contact for new referrals, patients, families, and referral partners, while also supporting light marketing and relationship-building efforts with existing referral sources.



Key Responsibilities:

1. Receive and process new hospice and home health referrals via phone, fax,
, and EMR systems.

2. Verify patient demographics, insurance eligibility, and medical documentation in compliance with HIPAA, Medicare, and California state regulations.

3. Coordinate and schedule initial assessments, admissions, and start-of-care visits with clinical staff.

4. Communicate clearly and compassionately with patients, families, physicians, hospitals, and referral partners.

5. Maintain accurate, complete, and confidential patient records in accordance with HIPAA privacy and security standards.

6. Track referral status, follow-ups, and outcomes to ensure timely admission or appropriate disposition.

7. Collaborate with clinical, billing, and administrative teams to ensure smooth transitions from intake to care delivery.

8. Provide updates to existing referral sources regarding patient status and admission outcomes.

9. Support minimal marketing activities such as maintaining referral lists, sending updates or materials to existing referral partners, and assisting with referral relationship management.

10. Assist with intake and referral reports as needed.



Requirements:

Previous experience in medical intake, hospice, home health, or healthcare coordination preferred.

Working knowledge of HIPAA compliance, patient confidentiality, and proper handling of PHI.

Strong verbal and written communication skills in English.

Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.

Ability to handle sensitive situations with professionalism, discretion, and empathy.

Proficiency with EMR systems and Microsoft Office or similar platforms.

Ability to multitask and work effectively in a fast-paced healthcare environment.



Preferred Qualifications:

Experience working with California-based hospice or home health agencies.

Familiarity with insurance verification, authorizations, physician orders, and Medicare guidelines.

Formal training or hands-on experience in HIPAA compliance and healthcare documentation standards.

Background in intake coordination, customer service, or referral management.

title: VA Scheduler - House Cleaning  US Company
description:
Position Title: Scheduler (with Customer Service Cross-Training)

Department: Operations

Employment Type: Full-time



Position Summary

The Scheduler is responsible for coordinating client appointments, maintaining accurate scheduling records, and ensuring seamless communication between clients, cleaners, and the sales team. This role requires strong organizational discipline, attention to detail, and proactive problem-solving to maintain operational continuity. The Scheduler will also be cross-trained in Customer Service functions to provide backup support when needed.



Key Responsibilities

Scheduling & Client Setup

Create and maintain new client profiles in company software.

Create client job records, including initial visits and recurring service frequency.

Verify that all customer information and system links are correctly completed.

Monitor new client intake and complete system setup promptly.

Create one-time job records for existing recurring clients when applicable.

Acquire company service zoning knowledge to support in the scheduling logistics and ensure logistics logic is applied when working on daily schedule routine. 

Schedule Management & Coordination

Maintain and update client scheduling.

Adjust schedules to accommodate time-off requests, last-minute client cancellations, or employee absences.

Analyze weekly schedule capacity and communicate availability to Sales for quoting and lead management.

Perform weekly planning for recurring monthly clients.



Client Communication

Confirm service setup and initial visit details with new clients.

Send visit confirmation messages for upcoming appointments.

Communicate scheduling changes to clients and staff when necessary.



Monitoring & Change Management

Review communications for cancellations or change requests, including:

Monday morning review of weekend change requests

Phone and Text messages from clients

Staff change in schedule 

Reassign or fill canceled appointments when possible.

Update scheduling records accordingly.



Reporting

Prepare and present a weekly scheduling report for operational meetings.

Review upcoming monthly cleanings to ensure readiness.

Create and provide any pertinent reports.



System & Process Maintenance

Maintain accurate records in company software and scheduling tools.

Ensure scheduling data integrity and operational transparency.



Customer Service Backup Responsibilities (Cross-Training)

Provide support for incoming client communications during peak periods or staff absence.

Assist with appointment inquiries, changes, and confirmations.

Maintain professional and solution-focused client interactions.

Escalate issues appropriately while preserving service continuity.



Required Skills & Qualifications

Experience with US House Cleaning Scheduling 

Strong organizational and time-management skills.

Ability to process critical thinking

High attention to detail and data accuracy.

Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Fluent English (conversational and written)

Professional written and verbal communication skills.

Problem-solving mindset with adaptability to last-minute changes.

Comfort working with scheduling and CRM systems

Customer-focused attitude and teamwork orientation.



Preferred Qualifications

Familiarity with CRM/scheduling platforms

Background in service-based business operations.

Spanish is a plus



Work Expectations

Consistent collaboration and good relationship with operations and sales teams.

Commitment to maintaining reliable scheduling workflows.

Pro activity in suggesting and initiating process improvements.



If you are interested, please record a brief video introducing yourself, your professional experience and anything you feel important to share. Applications without the video won't be considered.

title: Shopify Web Developer - CRO Specialist - Ecommerce Advertising Agency - Full Time 4b

In this role you will update Shopify theme templates, develop landing pages using various page builders and html/CSS frameworks, research/install various checkout/bundle applications, and implement A/B tests using testing tools like intelligems.

You will also report on test performance, and provide suggestions for next steps.

This is a full-time opportunity. Training will be provided and you will work directly with me.

dia buying and client strategy, to agency internal operations.

title: Customer Service Agent (Remote) – World Class eCommerce Brand
description:
We are one of the fastest growing eCommerce brands globally, trusted by hundreds of thousands of customers worldwide!



Our mission is to deliver high quality products and a seamless, heartfelt experience so that customers feel valued, heard, and inspired every time they interact with us.



Customer Service at our brand is not just a support function or a cost center — it's the core of our reputation and growth engine, where we turn customers into lifelong advocates.



As a Customer Service Agent, you'll be the voice of our brand, resolving issues, celebrating customer wins, and finding ways to surprise and delight. Every day is an opportunity to raise the bar and help shape a team that values growth, accountability, and kindness.



Key Responsibilities



You won't just answer tickets — you'll own 
nts of connection that strengthen our brand:

• Be the frontline hero for our customers through
, chat, and social support (no calls).

• Represent our values and tone in every
, chat, or social reply.

• Resolve order issues, shipping questions, returns, and product inquiries with clarity and empathy.

• Tag and categorize tickets accurately to power internal reporting and insights.

• Use platforms such as Richpanel, Shopify, and Notion to manage tickets efficiently.

• Follow up on escalations with urgency and professionalism.

• Track reopened conversations and duplicate tickets per internal SOPs to keep workflows seamless.

• Proactively highlight patterns or issues that need attention — your insights matter.

• Contribute ideas and documentation that make our processes even better.

• Uphold strict response time and resolution KPIs — we aim for lightning fast, heartfelt service.



Key Qualifications & Success Traits



• 1+ year of ecommerce customer support experience (Shopify experience a plus).

• Excellent written English with a friendly, confident tone — you'll be one of our brand voices.

• A growth mindset: you love feedback, learning, and getting better each day.

• Detail-oriented and accountable — you follow through and own your work.

• Proven reliability and discipline to thrive in a structured, high-expectation environment.

• Familiarity with customer service platforms (e.g., Richpanel, Gorgias, Zendesk) is a bonus.

• A stable internet connection and quiet workspace so you can deliver your best every shift.

• A genuine desire to be part of a world-class brand — we want people who are proud of the work they do.

• Availability to work full-time during the 12 pm – 9 pm PHT core shift (including standard break).



What Success Looks Like



• Customers leave interactions feeling heard, helped, and happier than when they arrived.

• Your tickets are handled quickly, accurately, and warmly, consistently meeting SLAs.

• You help create documentation, macros, or insights that improve the whole team.

• You take pride in representing a brand that stands for quality, care, and ambition.



Why Join Us?



• Be part of a fast-growing, globally loved brand on an exciting journey with clear plans to reach even greater milestones.

• Work remotely in a team that values kindness, ownership, and high standards — your ideas and contributions truly matter here.

• Enjoy competitive pay, regular performance reviews, and incentives for high performance.

• Join a culture built on operational excellence, innovation, and ambition. We invest in our people and celebrate growth.

• This is not just a support role; it's an opportunity to own and elevate the heartbeat of our customer experience as part of our growth engine.

• Enjoy the flexibility of a remote role while working in a high-performing international team.



How to Apply



If you're passionate about being part of a world-class eCommerce brand and customer service team, we'd love to hear from you!



To apply, please fill out our application form here: 



You'll upload your CV and complete our Customer Service Assessment Mission to showcase your skills. 



Only applications submitted through the Typeform will be considered.

title: Virtual Receptionist VA (Remote) | Fast Hiring – 24-Hour Review
description:
Important: Our application process moves quickly. If you are selected to proceed to the next interview stages, you must be able to respond promptly. Candidates who do not respond in a timely manner may be passed over.



We are currently seeking a Remote Spa Receptionist to manage inbound calls and book spa appointments. This is a long-term, performance-based position. Calls are recorded and reviewed regularly. Continued placement in this role depends on accuracy, professionalism, booking success, reliable attendance, no missed calls during scheduled shifts, quick response times, and the ability to professionally manage complaints and de-escalate dissatisfied clients.



To apply: Please copy the questions below, paste them into your reply, and answer each question thoroughly.



(1) Location & Utilities: Where in the Philippines are you currently located? How often have you experienced power or internet outages in the past year? Do you have backup solutions (e.g., generator, mobile hotspot) to ensure your work is not affected?



(2) Home Setup: Will you be working from home? Is your workspace quiet and suitable for handling calls? Do you have your own computer or laptop? Please attach a screenshot of your current internet speed.



(3) Availability & Speed: Can you consistently work seven days a week, six hours per day, and remain available for the full shift without missed calls? How quickly do you typically answer inbound calls? What is your current typing speed in words per minute?



(4) Call & Complaint Experience: Describe your experience with answering inbound calls, booking or scheduling appointments, customer service, following scripts, and handling complaints. Provide one real example of how you de-escalated an upset client and the outcome.



(5) Tools & Systems: What programs, tools, phone systems, or VoIP platforms have you used for handling calls? Do you have any issues with background noise, power outages, or internet interruptions? How do you take notes and log client interactions during calls?



(6) Voice Recordings: Please submit two short voice recordings. Recording one should demonstrate booking an appointment from 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM with a total amount of $60. Recording two should demonstrate handling a double charge for a 90-minute massage where the client sees two $90 charges and is requesting a refund. Your tone should be calm, polite, and professional.



(7) Current Job: Do you currently have another job? If yes, where, how many hours per week, and what are your roles and tasks in that job?



(8) Schedule, Pay & Start Date: Are you able to work seven days a week, six hours per day, during Central Standard Time (CST) hours without conflicts? What is your expected hourly or monthly rate? What is your earliest physical start date if hired?



(9) Marketing, Design & Video: Do you have experience with online marketing, creating content using Canva, or producing short video clips such as
 Reels or similar platforms? Please describe your skills and provide samples of your previous work if available.



(10) Fit, Skills & Experience: Briefly introduce yourself and explain why you would be a good fit for this role. Include what you believe is most important when handling spa clients over the phone, especially during complaints, and what other skills or areas you have a lot of experience in.

title: Product Marketer — Mobile Apps (UA & Competitor Research)
description:
We're AI Studio 10, a mobile app studio building AI-native consumer products. Our flagship, Bible & AI Chat, is one of many — we ship fast, test aggressively, and live or die by the numbers.

We're hiring a Product Marketer to own the two levers that matter most in subscription apps: understanding what's working in the market and turning that into paid users who convert. This is a role for someone who gets genuinely excited about reverse-engineering a top-grossing app's paywall, spotting a creative angle three weeks before it saturates, and building the UA machine that turns insight into installs.

What you'll own

Competitor research is the core of this role, not a side task. You'll live inside tools like Sensor Tower, data.ai, AppMagic, and Foreplay — tracking creative trends, paywall experiments, onboarding flows, and pricing moves across the AI consumer category and adjacent verticals. You'll build the intelligence layer that informs every product and UA decision we make.

On the UA side, you'll plan, launch, and optimise campaigns across Meta, TikTok, Apple Search Ads, and Google — from creative briefs through bidding strategy through LTV analysis. You'll work directly with our UA manager and creative producers, and your insights will drive the brief.

You'll also own positioning, ASO, and the narrative layer: store listings, screenshots, paywall copy, and the messaging that makes someone tap "subscribe" instead of closing the app.

What we're looking for

– 2–4 years in mobile app marketing, ideally on subscription consumer apps

– Obsessive about competitor research — you already have a teardown folder on your desktop

– Fluent with the UA stack: Meta, TikTok, ASA, AppsFlyer, paywall tooling (RevenueCat, Superwall, Adapty)

– Strong creative instinct — you can spot a winning ad angle and brief it clearly

– Comfortable with data: cohort analysis, LTV, payback, ROAS, not just surface-level dashboards

– Entrepreneurial. We're a studio, not an agency. You'll move fast and own outcomes.

Bonus points

– Experience in AI-native or faith/wellness/self-improvement categories

– Have shipped your own side project or written publicly about mobile marketing

– Comfortable with AI tooling for creative production and research workflows

The setup

Remote, fully global. We work async by default with overlap hours for collaboration. Compensation is competitive and includes performance upside tied to portfolio growth.

How to apply

Send us something that shows how you think — a competitor teardown you've done, a UA campaign breakdown, a creative angle you spotted early. A CV is fine too, but the work tells us more.

title: Outbound Lead Research & Messaging Specialist
description:
About Us

We're GonnaMakeIt (GMI) - a crypto project that's been around since 2021. Our founder, Tommy Worldpower, previously built Energi, still running since 2018.



Our main product is LP Bonds - a DeFi application that helps crypto projects keep their liquidity locked in forever, instead of watching it disappear when market conditions change. In return, users earn high yields and get an NFT that represents their position, which they can trade anytime. We're live on 7+ blockchains, partnered with major decentralized exchanges, and growing. We're early, lean, and moving fast.





What This Role Actually Is

95%+ of your day is cold outreach on X 
). You're finding crypto projects and ecosystems, identifying decision-makers, and hitting them with sharp, personalized DMs and public replies that start real conversations, not spam. When they reply, you research the project, set up a
 group with leadership, and hand off warm leads.

You're not waiting for leads to come to you. You're hunting.





Day-to-Day

- X Outreach (95%+): High-volume, high-quality cold DMs and strategic public replies. Multiple touches per lead (3-5 minimum). Messages that feel human, relevant, and impossible to ignore.

-
 Group Setup & Management: Positive reply ? you create a TG group, add the lead and LP Bonds leadership, drop project stats and context, and keep the group active. Setting up a group and going silent is not acceptable.

- Project Research: Every project that engages gets researched - tokenomics, liquidity structure, team, pain points. Leadership should never have to ask "who is this project?"

- Daily Reporting & Logging: Messages sent, replies received, project statuses, pipeline movement. Reports submitted on time, every day. If it's not logged, it didn't happen.

- AI-Augmented Workflow: You use AI to sharpen messaging and move faster - but your judgment and strategy are yours. AI-generated responses in team communication are not acceptable.





Non-Negotiables

- Weekly meetings are mandatory. No excuses, no ghosting.

- Communicate proactively. If you're pinged, you respond - not with a thumbs up emoji, with an actual reply. Silence kills deals and trust.

- Never self-close a lead. Your job is to keep doors open, not shut them.

- Track every project. Every lead, every status, every follow-up - updated daily. If a TG group exists, it stays active with context and updates. Letting projects go cold because you stopped tracking is highly discouraged.

- Own your pipeline. You don't just send messages and wait. You follow up, you re-engage, you escalate. A lead going cold on your watch is your problem.



You Must Have

- 3+ years cold outreach / DM experience (crypto or adjacent)

- 2+ years in crypto/Web3

- Strong X presence - active, credible account

- Sharp, concise writing that doesn't sound like a bot or a salesperson

- Self-starter - no hand-holding, no daily training

- Disciplined about tracking and reporting

-
 application experience 



Nice to Have

- DeFi / liquidity provision experience

- CoinGecko/CoinMarketCap familiarity

-
 community management experience





This Role Is NOT For You If

- You need scripts for every message

- You think 10 DMs a day is "high volume."

- You can't operate independently

- You treat outreach like spam instead of a conversation

- You go quiet when things get hard

- You think "waiting for reply" is a status update

title: Social Media Department Lead
description:
Social Media Manager & Strategist



About Us:

Sunset Solutions Marketing is a digital marketing agency exclusively serving DJ companies in the event industry. We handle websites, SEO, social media, and AI-powered automation for high-volume wedding, corporate, and event DJ operations. We are niche, strategic, and built for scale. The person who fills this role is joining at the ground level of something growing fast.



The Role:

We are looking for a Social Media Manager & Strategist to lead the social media presence for two accounts: the founder's personal brand and the agency brand. This is not an executor role. You will own the content calendar, bring creative direction, write in the founder's voice, and take initiative. The right person thinks before they create, asks smart questions, and delivers work that feels intentional — not templated.



As the agency grows and takes on DJ industry clients, you will expand into managing their social media accounts as well. You are building and leading the social media department. As your capacity is reached, a social media assistant will be brought on for you to lead and delegate to. You won't just be doing the work — you'll be building the department.



How We Work:

This is a retainer-style role, not a task queue. You manage your own time, work async, and don't need to be online at specific hours. What matters is that deadlines are met, communication is proactive, and the work speaks for itself. You won't be micromanaged — but you will be expected to own your scope completely.



Accounts & Platforms:

You will start by managing two accounts — the founder's personal brand and the agency brand — across Instagram and
. As the agency scales, DJ industry client accounts will be added to your scope. We support Instagram,
, YouTube, LinkedIn, and TikTok depending on client strategy.



Scope of Work:

Content calendar — you own it, build it, and maintain it. This is the first deliverable before any posts are created.

Content ideation — bring ideas based on brand voice, current trends, and the audience.

Canva graphics — create on-brand visuals. Brand guides and color palettes are provided. Bring aesthetic judgment, not just execution.

Captions — written in the founder's voice. You will have access to a brand voice guide, tone document, and a brand voice AI prompt.

Scheduling — batch and schedule posts in advance.

Engagement — respond to comments, engage with relevant accounts, comment on posts in the niche. Proactive, not passive. (DMs are handled by the founder.)

Reporting — basic monthly performance notes.



Post Frequency:

2 posts per week per account to start (~16 posts/month total)

Mix of Reels, carousels, and static posts — determined in the content calendar

Stories are handled by the founder — not part of this scope

A video editor is available for heavier editing needs



AI Proficiency:

AI is a core part of how this agency operates. You should be comfortable using AI tools for content ideation, caption drafting, and research. You don't need to be an expert, but you need to be willing to learn and integrate AI into your workflow. This will only become more important as the role grows.



What You Will Have Access To:

Brand guide and color palette

Tone and voice documents

Brand voice AI prompt for caption assistance

Content ideas and direction from the founder

Video editor for reel editing support

Scheduling and project management tools provided

*Specific tools are not set in stone — if you have preferred tools that get the job done better, we're open to the conversation.



Compensation & Hours:

Rate: $7.50/hr to start — increases to $8.50/hr when first client account is added

Hours: 10/week to start, grows with each new client

Payment: twice monthly via Wise (1st and 15th)

Rate grows with the role — this is not a static position



Start: paid two-week trial period before moving into a formal contract



Sunset Solutions Marketing is actively signing DJ company clients across the U.S. As each new client comes on board, their accounts are added to your scope — and your hours and pay increase with them.

title: T-shirt Graphic Designer (Apparel, Trends & Cultural Moments)
description:
About the Company



Hardcore Italians is a US-based e-commerce brand built for the Italian-American community. A large part of our revenue comes from apparel — t-shirts, hoodies, and similar products tied to cultural 
nts, sports, and Italian-American identity.



We run paid ads daily and test new designs constantly. What moves the needle isn't planned seasonal drops — it's capitalizing on real things happening right now. A big sports win. A viral movie. A 
nt in the news cycle that the Italian-American community is buzzing about. We need someone who wakes up every day thinking about this.



IMPORTANT — Please Read Before Applying



This is NOT a purely creative role.

This is a performance and output role. Your work will be measured by consistency, volume, and what sells.



You will NOT:

Wait to be told what to design every day

Treat this like a portfolio project

Debate feedback or creative direction



You WILL:

Deliver 10+ designs per week, consistently

Monitor trends and cultural 
nts daily — without being reminded

Bring ideas and opportunities proactively

Follow brand standards exactly, every time



If you're fast, culturally tuned in, and have zero ego around feedback — you'll do great here. If you need creative control or prefer slow, polished work — this is not the right fit.



What You'll Do



• Create 10+ new product designs per week — primarily t-shirts, hoodies, and similar apparel

• Monitor trends daily using Google Trends, social media, news, etc — and turn relevant 
nts into design concepts fast

• Stay on top of Italian-American culture, sports, pop culture crossovers, and viral 
nts that connect to our audience

• Identify opportunities the team hasn't spotted yet — if something big is happening and it has an Italian angle, you should already be on it

• Deliver print-ready files consistently — correct specs, right colors, right fonts, no back-and-forth on basic errors

• Maintain an organized design library in Google Drive

• Keep a running log of what's working and what isn't



The 
nts You'll Be Watching



This brand sits at the intersection of Italian heritage and American culture. Winning 
nts can come from anywhere:

– Italian & European Soccer — national team wins, Serie A milestones, major tournament 
nts

– US Sports / Italian Crossover — Baseball (WBC, Italian-American legends), Football, Hockey

title: Marketing Lead
description:
We are looking for a proactive and reliable Marketing Lead to oversee our marketing team and help drive consistent execution of marketing initiatives. This role requires someone with strong leadership capability, sound judgment in marketing and design decisions, and the ability to manage people effectively.



The ideal candidate has prior experience leading a team, understands marketing materials and brand presentation, and can guide the team in producing high-quality content and visuals. We are also looking for someone who demonstrates professionalism, accountability, and the ability to work under pressure while maintaining team alignment.



Responsibilities



1. Lead and manage the marketing team to ensure tasks and campaigns are delivered on time

2. Provide direction on marketing strategies, campaigns, and content development

3. Review and guide the creation of marketing materials such as brochures, flyers, digital signage, and website content

4. Work closely with designers and content writers to maintain brand consistency and quality

5. Assess team performance, provide feedback, and ensure accountability across the team

6. Coordinate marketing initiatives and ensure collaboration among tea
mbers

7. Ensure marketing deliverables align with company goals and brand standards

8. Support continuous improvement of marketing processes and team productivity

.

2. Strong leadership, decision-making, and people management skills

3. Familiarity with marketing materials and strong judgment in graphic and brand presentation is a plus

4. Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities

5. Strong communication and collaboration skills

6. Open to feedback and able to adapt quickly

7. Reliable, proactive, and highly accountable

8. Must not have other active clients to ensure full focus on the role



Work Schedule



Pacific Time: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM

Time Tracker will be used during work hours

Hardcore Italians is a US-based e-commerce brand built for the Italian-American community. A large part of our revenue comes from apparel — t-shirts, hoodies, and similar products tied to cultural 
nts, sports, and Italian-American identity.



We run paid ads daily and test new designs constantly. What moves the needle isn't planned seasonal drops — it's capitalizing on real things happening right now. A big sports win. A viral movie. A 
nt in the news cycle that the Italian-American community is buzzing about. We need someone who wakes up every day thinking about this.

This is NOT a purely creative role.

This is a performance and output role. Your work will be measured by consistency, volume, and what sells.

Treat this like a portfolio project

Debate feedback or creative direction

nts daily — without being reminded

title: Virtual Assistant for Folly
description:
WHO WE ARE

We don’t just build dream homes and mansions— we create sanctuaries in nature where artists, athletes, and visionaries come to disconnect from the noise, recharge, and find inspiration to create what’s next.



WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS

- Starting at Php 45K+ (85K when you included our numerous benefits – Free Unemployment Insurance, Bonuses, etc.)

- Low stress, no burnout

- HAVE FUN at our team-building events in BGC with our Beverly Hills & Manila teams. We're talking about haunted houses, yoga, muay thai, painting, Korean BBQ, rooftop happy hours, dancing, and karaoke!



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

- Customer Support: Help musicians, athletes, celebrities, and top executives on chat,
, and phones with easy, low stress questions like booking rooms or general info about their stay.

title: Sales Closer – Car Rental & Leasing Business
description:
About Sin City Rides LV

Sin City Rides LV is a Las Vegas-based car rental company offering a range of vehicles from economy to luxury. We cater to personal users, long-term lessees, and gig economy drivers such as those working with Uber, Lyft, and DoorDash. With flexible terms, no credit checks, and a commitment to premium customer service, we simplify and streamline the rental experience.



As part of our continued growth, we are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Sales Closer to join our remote team. This role plays a critical part in converting qualified leads into finalized rental or lease agreements and supporting our expanding operations.



Position Overview

The Sales Closer will be responsible for handling warm, qualified leads and guiding them through the final stages of our rental or leasing process. This role requires excellent communication skills, strong sales experience, and a results-driven mindset. The ideal candidate is professional, persuasive, and committed to delivering a seamless client experience.



Key Responsibilities

1. Convert pre-qualified leads into successful rental or lease agreements by following up promptly and closing sales effectively.

2. Engage with clients via phone,
, text, and messaging platforms to understand their vehicle needs and provide appropriate solutions.

3. Confidently handle client objections and clearly explain the terms, programs, and benefits.

4. Guide clients through all required documentation, ensuring completeness and accuracy.

5. Maintain accurate records of client interactions, bookings, and performance metrics.

6. Collaborate with the Lead Generation and Operations teams to optimize the sales process and improve lead quality.

7. Provide feedback for continuous process improvement.



Qualifications

Proven experience in sales, particularly in rental, auto leasing, real estate, or other service-based industries.

Demonstrated ability to close deals and meet or exceed sales targets.

Strong interpersonal and negotiation skills.

Ability to work independently and maintain professionalism in a remote environment.

Familiarity with CRM tools and sales tracking platforms is an advantage.

Social media content creation experience is a plus, but not required.



Compensation and Benefits

Commission-based compensation per successful rental or lease agreement

Flexible, remote work environment

Opportunity to grow within a fast-expanding and dynamic rental business

Performance-based incentives and advancement opportunities



How to Apply

Interested applicants should send the following to 
 with the subject line:

"Sales Closer Application – Sin City Rides LV"

1. An updated resume
